package.name: bag_recorder_node
package.description:
bag_recorder_node — hosts /bag_recorder/start + /bag_recorder/stop services so Mission Control's web backend can trigger ros2 bag recordings INSIDE dv_pipeline_stack, where the recorder shares the SHM-tuned Fast DDS context with the publishers.
Why not record from mc_backend directly: mc_backend is forced to FASTDDS_BUILTIN_TRANSPORTS=UDPv4 so its StartMission action client works cross-container. UDP fragmentation reassembly drops most of the 1.4 MB /lidar/Lidar1 scans (96% loss measured in #465). Running the recorder in dv_pipeline_stack avoids that entirely.
Always-on (started from pipeline.launch.py alongside ifssim_bridge + foxglove_bridge), single-recording-at-a-time, writes to the container's /tmp during recording and moves to the bind-mounted /bags on stop so the streaming hot path stays off macOS virtiofs.
See docs/autonomy_pipeline.md and tools/mission_control/backend/ bag_recorder.py (the service client side).
